phase.name: Phase 1: bin/griot CLI + obvious wrapper kills

contract.goal: Port the two stdin-driven griot scripts (operator-checks.ts handling verify-rubric/log-intervention; mediate-panel.ts handling judge-panel verdict aggregation) to verb handlers under .claude/cli/verbs/griot/. Extend GriotCliContext with an optional stdin field; capture process.stdin eagerly in the dispatcher main() when not TTY. Byte-equivalent behavior to the legacy scripts. D2 of 4 in Phase 1.
acceptance_criteria: operatorChecksVerb(rest, ctx) exported from .claude/cli/verbs/griot/operator-checks.ts; mode from rest[0]; both verify-rubric and log-intervention modes preserved with identical output shape.
mediatePanelVerb(_rest, ctx) exported from .claude/cli/verbs/griot/mediate-panel.ts; same parse/tally/tiebreak logic as legacy; JSON.stringify(output, null, 2) body.
GriotCliContext extended with optional stdin?: string; .claude/cli/griot.ts main() populates ctx.stdin via readFileSync(0, 'utf8') when !process.stdin.isTTY, else empty string.
GRIOT_VERBS contains use, operator-checks, mediate-panel; help text registered for all three.
Sibling tests adapted from legacy: 19 operator-checks tests + 17 mediate-panel tests, all passing; 2 spawnSync smoke tests added to griot.test.ts for stdin piping end-to-end.
npm run lint clean; npm test clean; byte parity confirmed via diff against legacy scripts on representative fixtures.
rules_applied: Preserve external JSON schema vocabulary (snake_case fields like round_num, judge_id, consensus_verdict — out of naming evaluator's lane per its carve-out).
disqualifiers: Change the output shape of either verb (panel JSON structure, ok:true/false shape, error message text).
Diverge from bin/draft's flat-verb dispatcher architecture.

actions: Wrote .claude/cli/verbs/griot/operator-checks.ts (75 lines, ports verify-rubric + log-intervention; mode from rest[0]; JSON from ctx.stdin; fail() returns {stderr, exitCode: 1}).

Wrote .claude/cli/verbs/griot/mediate-panel.ts (269 lines, ports validateInput/parseSingleVerdict/tallyVerdicts/pickMajority/detectTierSplit/applyTiebreak; ValidationError class replaces the legacy fail-and-exit pattern; main verb composes Output and returns JSON.stringify body).
Extended GriotCliContext with optional stdin?: string field; preserves backward compat for existing use verb / tests.
Registered both verbs in GRIOT_VERBS map.
Updated .claude/cli/griot.ts main() to read stdin eagerly via readFileSync(0, 'utf8') when !process.stdin.isTTY (TTY check prevents blocking on interactive invocations).
Updated VERBS help-text registry with entries for operator-checks and mediate-panel.

Wrote operator-checks.test.ts (19 tests adapted from legacy: mode dispatch, verify-rubric exact/diff/missing-file/missing-fields, log-intervention create/append/parent-dir/multiple/arbitrary records).

Wrote mediate-panel.test.ts (17 tests adapted from legacy: input validation, round-1 unanimity, round-1 non-unanimity, round-2 supermajority, round-2 tiebreak fires, round-2 tiebreak no-fire, tier-split detection, errored verdicts).
Added 2 spawnSync smoke tests to griot.test.ts for end-to-end stdin handling via bin/griot.

Verified parity: diff <(bin/griot operator-checks verify-rubric < x) <(node .claude/scripts/griot/operator-checks.ts verify-rubric < x) → exit 0; same for log-intervention; same for mediate-panel on a 3-judge fixture.
Ran npm test: 37 files / 499 tests pass (was 35/461 after D1; +2 files +38 tests).
Invoked /guild-validate with evaluator-contract-fit + evaluator-naming; both VERDICT: approved; aggregated approved.

scope: Port operator-checks verb (stdin-driven; verify-rubric + log-intervention modes).
Extend GriotCliContext with optional stdin field.
Update dispatcher main() to capture process.stdin when not TTY.
Sibling tests for both new verbs + 2 spawnSync smoke tests.
changes_since_previous: Builds on D1 (checkin 01.json). New files alongside D1's verb. Dispatcher modified to register new verbs and capture stdin. Legacy scripts still preserved.
notes_for_pr: D2 of 4 in Phase 1. PR refresh deferred until phase close (after D4).

Byte-for-byte parity verified against the legacy scripts for all three representative fixtures (operator-checks verify-rubric error-path, operator-checks log-intervention append, mediate-panel 3-judge aggregation).

stdin handling: dispatcher reads stdin eagerly via readFileSync(0, 'utf8') when !process.stdin.isTTY. TTY check prevents bin/griot --help / use from blocking when invoked interactively without a pipe.

ValidationError class added to mediate-panel.ts — replaces the legacy fail-and-exit pattern (which directly called process.exit from inside a validator). The verb-handler shape requires returning DispatchResult, so validation errors throw ValidationError and the top-level handler catches and converts. Same surface for the caller (stderr + exit 1).

snake_case fields on Input/Output types (round_num, judge_id, consensus_verdict, etc.) are preserved verbatim — they're the external JSON schema crossing the stdin boundary, not internal style choices. Naming evaluator's external-schema carve-out applies.
